---
applyTo: "**/*.{md,mdx,txt}"
---

# Documentation Instructions

These instructions apply to Markdown and documentation files.

## Documentation Quality

- Use clear, practical language.
- Keep documentation accurate and up to date.
- Prefer concise explanations with enough detail to be useful.
- Avoid documenting behavior that does not exist.
- Do not invent requirements, APIs, commands, or architecture decisions.

## Requirements Documentation

The original requirements may be provided as a PDF.

When converting requirements to Markdown:

- Preserve business meaning.
- Organize content clearly.
- Identify unclear or conflicting requirements.
- List open questions.
- Do not fill major gaps with assumptions.
- Ask clarifying questions before making important decisions.

## Architecture Documentation

Ask before creating or updating architecture documentation or ADRs.

When approved, document:

- Decision context
- Options considered
- Chosen approach
- Trade-offs
- Risks
- Consequences
- Follow-up actions

## README Updates

Update README or setup documentation when changes affect:

- Local setup
- Build commands
- Test commands
- Environment variables
- Project structure
- API usage
- Development workflow

## API Documentation

- Keep API documentation synchronized with implementation.
- Document request and response behavior clearly.
- Include important validation and error behavior.
- Do not document unsupported behavior.

## Technical Writing Style

- Use headings and lists for readability.
- Prefer examples when helpful.
- Keep terminology consistent.
- Separate confirmed facts from open questions or future work.
